description: The plant produces melons which are large modified berries called a pepo. They are rounded to oval mottled green with darker green rind. Black, cream or mottled colored elliptic seeds. Flesh general red or pink but can also be yellowish.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: seasonally dry tropical
care.tips: Similar plants: Capsicum annuum | Native to: NCB, AGW SJ, NWY OO, GEO, GHA

care.light_guide: Climate: seasonally dry tropical
care.tips: Native to: TEX OO, NGA OO, AND, NORTHERN SOUTH AMERICA, AUSTRALASIA

description: The  vegetable  is botanically a fruit– it is a pepo, a berry with a hard rind.  Long and cylindrical, starting out prickly when young and smoothing out to a bumpy surface as it matures.  Length and girth can vary based on cultivar and culinary purpose but grow at least 3 inches long.  Some varieties are bred to be seedless. The surface of the fruit is covered in hairs, spines or warts.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: seasonally dry tropical
care.tips: Similar plants: Cucumis sativus | Native to: BZE BA, TEX OO, NORTHERN SOUTH AMERICA, AUSTRALASIA, NORTHEASTERN USA
common_problems: There are pest and disease problems, such as cucumber beetles and aphids, that can generally be managed through good cultural practices.
common_pests: There are pest and disease problems, such as cucumber beetles and aphids, that can generally be managed through good cultural practices.

description: Hairs cover the oblong fruit giving the species the nickname  “hedgehog cucumber”. Fruits appear in November to January. They start out green and turn yellow when mature and contain many seeds.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: desert or dry shrubland
care.tips: Similar plants: Cucurbita argyrosperma | Native to: BZE PB, IND GO, HAW JI, ERI, BZE FN

care.tips: Native to: NCB, ERI, SWZ OO, MYA, MLY

description: Cucumis melo, also known as melon or true melon, is a species of Cucumis that has been developed into many cultivated varieties. The fruit is a pepo. The flesh is either sweet or bland, with or without an aroma, and the rind can be smooth, ribbed, wrinkled, or netted. The species is sometimes referred to as muskmelon.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: seasonally dry tropical
care.tips: Similar plants: Cucumis sativus | Native to: NORTHERN SOUTH AMERICA, JAP SH, GUI, SAM WS, MLI
common_problems: Plants in the cucumber family are susceptible  to cucumber beetles and wilt, powdery mildew, downy mildew, gummy stem blight, and anthracnose disease.
common_pests: Plants in the cucumber family are susceptible  to cucumber beetles and wilt, powdery mildew, downy mildew, gummy stem blight, and anthracnose disease.

care.tips: Native to: MIDDLE EUROPE, NSW, NCS ST, NCS KC, NCS IN

description: The cucumber is a widely-cultivated creeping vine plant in the family Cucurbitaceae that bears cylindrical to spherical fruits, used as culinary vegetables. Considered an annual plant, there are three main types of cucumber—slicing, pickling, and seedless.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: seasonally dry tropical
care.tips: Good companions: Beans, Corn, Dill, Nasturtium, Oregano, Peppers, Radish, Sunflowers, Tansy | Similar plants: Cucurbita pepo | Native to: NCB, NCS ST, NWY OO, GEO, GEO OO
common_problems: There are pest and disease problems that can generally be managed through good cultural practices.
common_pests: There are pest and disease problems that can generally be managed through good cultural practices.

description: Various shapes and colors of fruits are used for decorative purposes and seeds are edible.  Often has a lovely striped and speckled pattern.

care.tips: Similar plants: Cucurbita maxima | Native to: MXS, MXE AG, IND GO, IND PO, MXG

description: A pepo (berry with a hard rind) that is mottled white and green.  The inner flesh is white with black seeds.  Known for its incredible storage qualities and for the uniform size and color.

care.tips: Similar plants: Cucurbita argyrosperma | Native to: AGW SJ, MXG, AGW, SPA GI, ASS

care.tips: Native to: NEV, VRG OO, TEX OO, KAN OO, MXS CL

description: Soft or hard-shelled berries called pepos come in a variety of colors, shapes, and sizes and are harvested in the fall.  The stems of the fruits generally have a soft corky texture compared to other species.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: subtropical
care.tips: Good companions: Beans, Borage, Calendula, Corn, Lovage, Marigold, Nasturtium, Oregano, Radish, Tansy | Similar plants: Cucurbita argyrosperma | Native to: NCB, AGW SJ, NWY OO, GEO, GEO OO

description: A type of berry called a pepo that has a hard rind. Fruits may be long or round, large or small, smooth or warty. Comes in a variety of colors, shapes, and sizes.  Fruits and seeds are both edible.

care.watering_guide: Propagation: sow seeds in pots in a greenhouse

description: Cucurbita pepo is a cultivated plant of the genus Cucurbita. It yields varieties of winter squash and pumpkin, but the most widespread varieties belong to the subspecies Cucurbita pepo subsp. pepo, called summer squash.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: subtropical
care.tips: Good companions: Beans, Borage, Calendula, Corn, Lovage, Marigold, Nasturtium, Oregano, Radish, Tansy | Similar plants: Cucurbita argyrosperma | Native to: STH OO, AGW SJ, NWY OO, WAK, NWH OO
care.toxicity_note: Toxic to Dogs, Non-Toxic to Cats, Non-Toxic to Horses.

description: Ecballium is a genus of flowering plants in the family Cucurbitaceae containing a single species, Ecballium elaterium, also called the squirting cucumber. Its unusual common name derives from the ripe fruit squirting a stream of mucilaginous liquid containing its seeds as a means of seed dispersal, an example of rapid plant movement.

Fruit (a pepo to 3 feet long) has a smooth, light green skin when young, but matures to yellow or light brown. Mature fruits take a variety of shapes, including rounded, dumbbell-shaped, bottle-shaped or crookneck-shaped. The fruit is fleshy and dry but not split open.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.tips: Similar plants: Cucurbita argyrosperma

description: Luffa aegyptiaca, the sponge gourd, Egyptian cucumber or Vietnamese luffa, is an annual species of vine cultivated for its fruit, native to South and Southeast Asia.

care.tips: Similar plants: Cucurbita argyrosperma

care.watering_guide: Propagation: Seed - direct sow, Seed - transplant
care.tips: Good companions: Allium cepa, Amaranthus cruentus, Anethum graveolens, Asparagus, Broad Bean, Broccoli, Cauliflower, Celery, Common Sunflower, Common bean | Keep apart: Potato, Sage

description: Momordica charantia is a tropical and subtropical vine of the family Cucurbitaceae, widely grown in Asia, Africa, and the Caribbean for its edible fruit. Its many varieties differ substantially in the shape and bitterness of the fruit.
